# SRC004 - The Long and the Short of It

## Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Section | Advertising Effectiveness |
| Authors / source | Binet & Field |
| Year | 2013 |
| Source type | Effectiveness case database / practitioner research |
| Quality | Medium-High |
| URL | [https://ipa.co.uk/knowledge/publications-reports/the-long-and-the-short-of-it-balancing-short-and-long-term-marketing-strategies](https://ipa.co.uk/knowledge/publications-reports/the-long-and-the-short-of-it-balancing-short-and-long-term-marketing-strategies) |

## Bias / limitation

IPA case database; award/case selection bias but extremely useful.

## Core takeaway

Brand-building and activation work differently; short-term metrics alone can mislead strategy.

## How the marketing agent should use it

Use to force every campaign plan to label whether it is activation, brand-building, or a hybrid.

## Tactics unlocked

- Convert source insight into a campaign hypothesis.
- Translate the hypothesis into one ad angle, one landing-page proof section, and one measurement plan.
- Record whether the tactic worked in `memory/tactic_performance.md`.

## Agent instruction

When this source is relevant, write one sentence explaining **why this source applies to the buyer state** before using it.

## Research extraction template

```markdown
### Source extraction

- Source ID: SRC004
- What it changes about our strategy:
- What it proves:
- What it does not prove:
- Ad tactic suggested:
- Landing page proof suggested:
- Measurement implication:
- Follow-up source needed:
```
